<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py-->
{{Expand Japanese|伊勢神社 (岡山市北区)}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Ise Shrine
| native_name = 伊勢神社
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes
| mapframe-point = none
| map_type = Japan
| map_caption = Ise Shrine
| coordinates = {{coord|34.674646|133.929406}}
| map_relief = 1
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| website = {{Official website|http://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjashi-kensaku/jsearch3jinjashi.php?jinjya=6510}}
}}

{{nihongo|'''Ise Shrine'''|伊勢神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bizen Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}}
* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}
* {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
* {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}
* {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
* {{ill|Q135419779|ltq=Q135419779|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}}

== street address (P6375) ==

[[JA:伊勢神社 (岡山市北区)]]

{{wikidata link|Q135041208}}

== Japanese content ==
{{Citation needed|date=December 2025}}
{{Shinto shrine
| Name = Ise Shrine
| Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Okayama City}}
| Location = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama City|Q200078|qid=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama City)|Q855478|qid=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}} Bancho 2-11-20
| Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
| Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}} · {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}}
| Sacred object = 
| Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
| Founded = 
| Main hall = 
| Alternative names = 
| Pilgrimage sites, etc. = 
| Annual festival = October 16–17
| Rituals = 
| Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ise Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Ise Shrine''' (いせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama City|Q200078|qid=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama City)|Q855478|qid=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its common name is '''Ise-no-miya''' (いせのみや).

=== Enshrined deities ===
; Principal deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}} · {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Ise Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=6510|access-date=December 21, 2025}}</ref>

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded by the imperial princess {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyosukiiri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. As a {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|qid=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}}, it has a history of more than two thousand years. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In the same era, in year 15 (1882), it was promoted to the rank of Prefectural shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Shrine Association – Ise Shrine|url=https://jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=6510|access-date=December 19, 2025}}</ref>.

=== Festivals ===
* Kinen-sai (2nd Sunday of May)
* Summer Festival ({{ill|lt=July 29|Q2717|qid=Q2717|1=July 29}})
* Grand Annual Festival ({{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|qid=Q2959|1=October 20}} · {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|qid=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}})

=== On-site information ===
; Address
* Bancho 2-11-20, {{ill|lt=Kita Ward (Okayama City)|Q855478|qid=Q855478|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama City|Q200078|qid=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}
; Access
* Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Station|Q801293|qid=Q801293|1=Okayama Station}} (about a 16-minute walk)
